params.name: Poincaré Conjecture
params.description:
On Feb 3, 1904 AD, French mathematician Henri Poincaré proposed this problem.
The Poincaré Conjecture is a topological problem that describes a closed three-dimensional space, if each closed curve can be shrunk to a point, then this space must be a three-dimensional sphere. Later, this conjecture was extended to higher-dimensional spaces. It is one of the seven Millennium Prize Problems, along with the Hodge conjecture and the Riemann hypothesis, and is considered one of the most challenging …
